sourceFiles.name: editor (current)
requirements

fix.title: Resolve mass-geometry-material conflict by defining a hollow cube with feasible dimensions and mass limit
fix.description: This fix removes the hard conflict by replacing the ambiguous and infeasible geometry/weight statements with a physically consistent set of requirements. The shape is clarified using valid dimensions, the construction is explicitly defined as hollow with a wall thickness, and the maximum mass is revised to a value compatible with a 1 m side-length enclosure made from 2024 aluminium. This creates a feasible design space while preserving the original intent to constrain shape, material, and weight.

changes.description: Clarified the shape requirement by replacing the dimensionally invalid phrase "perfect cube of 1m^2" with an unambiguous cube side length.
Added an explicit construction requirement to specify that the cube is hollow and bounded by a realistic wall thickness, resolving the missing artifact identified in the finding recommendation.
Normalized the material wording while retaining 2024 aluminium as the required material.
Revised the mass requirement from an infeasible 1 kg maximum to a feasible 35 kg maximum for a 1 m hollow 2024 aluminium cube enclosure.

fix.title: Clarify cube geometry by replacing area with side length
fix.description: This fix removes the internal contradiction in `requirements::Shape` by replacing the ambiguous and dimensionally inconsistent phrase `perfect cube of 1m^2` with an unambiguous cube size stated as side length. A side length directly defines a cube's geometry and eliminates the requirement-level hard conflict identified in the review finding.

changes.description: Updated the Shape requirement text to use an unambiguous cube dimension expressed as side length instead of the conflicting area unit `1m^2`.

fix.title: Add explicit conflict resolution hierarchy and rationale for shape-material-mass requirements
fix.description: This fix resolves CONFLICT-003 by preserving the existing requirements and adding an explicit stakeholder-approved resolution requirement plus refine relationships that define priority if the conflict remains unresolved. The model now states that safety/handling mass limit takes precedence, material is secondary, and geometry is lowest priority, with rationale directing trade studies and requirement relaxation decisions. This provides documented priority and resolution guidance required by the criterion.

changes.description: Added an explicit conflict-resolution requirement that documents priority ordering and rationale for how the apparent contradiction among mass, material, and geometry is to be resolved.
Added a concrete requirement instance with refine relationships to the conflicting requirements so the priority applies directly to Shape, Material, and Weight in the model.

results.name: 05_conflicting_requirements
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: The model specifies a very simple physical system with requirements on shape, material, and maximum weight.
- Operational domain: Generic physical product / mechanical domain; no specific industry context is stated.
- Model scope: Very small requirements-only fragment. The two files contain the same package and appear to duplicate the same requirement definitions.
- Known scope limitations: No structure, behavior, interfaces, allocations, parametrics, units model, requirement relationships, or rationale/resolution elements are present. Checks depending on state machines, activities, interfaces, allocations, timing models, or parent-child requirement decomposition can only be assessed for explicit absence, not detailed contradiction analysis.

ISSUE [CONFLICT-001]
Element(s):     requirements::Shape, requirements::Material, requirements::Weight
Check:          1. Numeric Constraint Conflicts
Finding:        The stated shape/size and material imply a physical mass far greater than the allowed maximum weight. A perfect cube of approximately 1 m characteristic size made of 2024 aluminium cannot simultaneously weigh no more than 1 kg.
Evidence:       Shape: "The system shall be a perfect cube of 1m^2." Material: "The system shall be made of 2024 Aluminium." Weight: "The system shall weigh no more than 1kg." Even if the shape text is interpreted generously as a cube with 1 m side length or 1 m^2 face area, a solid aluminium cube would have mass orders of magnitude above 1 kg.
Conflict Type:  Hard
Score:          0.1  -  The requirements set is effectively unsatisfiable as written, blocking any valid physical design solution.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The contradiction is strong, but certainty is limited by the malformed unit/expression "cube of 1m^2", which is dimensionally ambiguous and requires interpretation.
Impact:         Physical design trades, sizing, and compliance assessment are blocked because no plausible interpretation of the geometric and material requirements yields a 1 kg system.
Recommendation: Clarify the geometry with valid dimensions/units, specify whether the object is solid or hollow, and revise either the material or mass limit so that a feasible design space exists.

ISSUE [CONFLICT-002]
Element(s):     requirements::Shape
Check:          4. Requirement-Level Conflicts
Finding:        The shape requirement is internally inconsistent because it specifies a three-dimensional object ("perfect cube") using an area unit ("1m^2"), creating a contradiction in the requirement itself and preventing unambiguous interpretation against the other requirements.
Evidence:       Shape: "The system shall be a perfect cube of 1m^2."
Conflict Type:  Hard
Score:          0.2  -  The requirement cannot be evaluated consistently, which undermines conflict analysis and downstream design interpretation.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Designers cannot determine the intended cube size, so mass, volume, manufacturability, and compliance analyses become ambiguous or invalid.
Recommendation: Replace "1m^2" with an unambiguous cube dimension such as side length, volume, or clearly stated face area with derived geometry.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Behavioural Conflicts  -  No behavioral model elements (states, transitions, activities, actions, sequencing, deadlines, or liveness constraints) are present, so no behavioral conflicts were identifiable within this model fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Interface & Protocol Conflicts  -  No ports, connections, flows, protocols, or interface specifications are present, so there is no interface/protocol conflict evidence to assess in this model fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Allocation Conflicts  -  No functions, physical components, processors, software allocations, or environmental allocation constraints are modeled, so allocation conflict checks do not apply here.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Safety vs. Functional Conflicts  -  No safety requirements, functional behavior requirements, watchdog constraints, or operational modes are modeled, so no explicit safety-functional conflict can be identified.

ISSUE [CONFLICT-003]
Element(s):     requirements::Shape, requirements::Material, requirements::Weight
Check:          7. Priority & Resolution
Finding:        The model contains an apparent unresolved conflict among geometry, material, and mass requirements, but provides no priority ordering, rationale, satisfy/refine relationship, or note explaining how the conflict is to be resolved.
Evidence:       Present requirements: "perfect cube of 1m^2", "made of 2024 Aluminium", and "weigh no more than 1kg." No priority, rationale, satisfy, or refine elements are shown anywhere in the provided model.
Conflict Type:  Latent
Score:          0.4  -  The unresolved contradiction is likely to force ad hoc stakeholder interpretation and wrong design decisions if left unmanaged.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Teams lack a basis for deciding whether to relax mass, geometry, or material, so requirement negotiation and verification planning are likely to diverge.
Recommendation: Add explicit priority/resolution guidance, such as a stakeholder-approved rationale, trade-study note, or requirement hierarchy stating which requirement governs if the conflict remains.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| CONFLICT-001 | 0.1 | MEDIUM | Shape/size, aluminium material, and 1 kg maximum mass are physically incompatible. |
| CONFLICT-002 | 0.2 | HIGH | The shape requirement is internally inconsistent because a cube is specified using area units. |
| CONFLICT-003 | 0.4 | HIGH | The model contains no documented priority or rationale to resolve the identified requirement conflict. |

Overall Assessment Score: 0.23
Model Status: Critical deficiencies - major rework required before this model can be used.
